title: Startup Accelerator in UAE 2026: MBRIF Program for High-Growth Innovators (Free & No Equity)
summary: If you are an entrepreneur or innovator looking to scale your startup and expand into new markets, the MBRIF Accelerator Program offers a powerful opportunity. Designed to support high-potential innovators, this program provides tailored guidance, access to industry experts, and strategic resources—all at no cost and without taking equity. But is this program right for you? Here’s everything you need to know. Is This Program Right for You? Before applying, it’s important to evaluate whether your innovation aligns with the program’s core focus and eligibility criteria. 1. Priority Sectors The accelerator is particularly interested in innovations within the following sectors: Technology Education Clean Energy Water Transport Health Space If your solution falls within any of these industries, you are already a strong candidate. 2. UAE Market Focus The program is designed for innovators who: Are currently based in the United Arab Emirates, or Are interested in establishing a presence in the UAE This makes it especially valuable for startups looking to expand into the Middle East and tap into a rapidly growing innovation ecosystem. 3. Startup Stage Requirement To qualify, your startup should be: Already in the market stage or beyond Past the idea or prototype phase This ensures that participants are ready to scale and benefit from advanced support and market access. 4. Market Potential Your innovation should demonstrate: Strong scalability The potential to create significant impact The program prioritizes solutions that can drive meaningful change both regionally and globally. Why You Should Apply If you are building a solution that addresses real-world challenges and are ready to scale, the MBRIF Accelerator can help you: Expand into the UAE market Strengthen your business model Access high-level expertise Accelerate your growth trajectory It is particularly beneficial for founders who lack access to industry insights, networks, and resources needed to scale effectively. The MBRIF Accelerator Program is more than just an accelerator—it is a launchpad for innovators who are serious about building impactful, scalable solutions. With its no-cost, no-equity model and tailored support, it stands out as one of the most founder-friendly programs available today. If your startup aligns with the program’s focus areas and you are ready to grow, this could be the opportunity that takes your innovation to the next level. About the MBRIF Accelerator Program The MBRIF Accelerator is a government-backed initiative focused on empowering the next generation of changemakers. It identifies and supports innovators with high growth potential, helping them thrive within the UAE and expand beyond. What Makes This Accelerator Unique? Personalized Support Unlike many traditional accelerators, this program offers a customized journey tailored to your startup’s specific needs. Whether you need market insights, strategic partnerships, or operational guidance, the support is designed around you. Zero Cost, Zero Equity One of the most attractive features of the program is its structure: No participation fees No equity taken There are no hidden terms—just pure support to help your business grow. Access to a Strong Ecosystem Participants gain access to: Industry experts Strategic partners Valuable market connections This ecosystem is designed to accelerate your growth and open doors that would otherwise be difficult to access. Collaborative Approach The program emphasizes collaboration, working closely with innovators to co-design solutions and strategies that align with their goals. How to Apply Interested innovators are encouraged to apply directly through the official program platform . Be sure to prepare clear details about your innovation, its market potential, and your expansion goals—especially regarding the UAE market. Take the next step and position your startup for global impact. Discover more similar opportunities here. title: Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship 2027 in Germany: €30,000 Funded Opportunity for Global Sustainability Leaders
summary: The Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship 2027 , offered by the Research Institute for Sustainability (RIFS) at the GFZ Helmholtz Centre in Potsdam, Germany, is now open for applications. This prestigious international fellowship is designed for mid-career professionals who are actively contributing to sustainable development and have the potential to drive transformative change across sectors. Named after Prof. Dr. Klaus Töpfer, a global leader in sustainability and the founding director of the Institute for Advanced Sustainability Studies (now RIFS), this fellowship supports individuals committed to bridging science, policy, society, and innovation. This opportunity provides financial support, institutional backing, and a collaborative research environment to develop impactful sustainability projects with long-term global relevance. About the Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship The Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship is part of the RIFS Fellow Programme, which focuses on interdisciplinary, transdisciplinary, and co-creative research to advance sustainability transitions. The fellowship aims to: Support innovative thinkers and practitioners working on sustainability Encourage collaboration across sectors such as science, policy, business, arts, and civil society Promote projects that generate long-term societal impact Strengthen global dialogue on sustainability transformation The fellowship will run for up to one year , beginning in the first quarter of 2027 , and requires fellows to work on-site in Potsdam, Germany. What the Fellowship Offers The programme provides comprehensive financial and institutional support to ensure fellows can focus on impactful work. Key Benefits Monthly stipend to support living expenses Additional financial compensation for relocation and living costs in Germany A discretionary project budget of up to €30,000 , which can be used for: Travel expenses Research and publication costs Organizing workshops and events Artistic or project production costs Access to a world-class research environment at RIFS Opportunities to collaborate with international experts and institutions Programme Structure and Flexibility The fellowship is designed to accommodate different professional and personal circumstances. Duration: Up to 12 months Flexible structure: Can be divided into multiple intervals if necessary Location: On-site in Potsdam, Germany Engagement: Fellows are expected to actively contribute to the institute’s research and activities Who Should Apply The fellowship is targeted at highly talented mid-career professionals with a strong background in sustainability-related work. Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Have at least 10 years of professional experience in sustainability-related fields Demonstrate the potential to act as a change agent Have experience working across different countries and cultures Present an innovative project with long-term impact Applicants may come from diverse sectors, including: Science and academia Public policy and governance Civil society and NGOs Business and private sector Arts and creative industries Project Requirements Applicants must submit a well-developed project proposal that includes: Project title and objectives Research or activity focus Work plan and timeline Expected outcomes and long-term impact Required resources and budget considerations The project should demonstrate the ability to bridge different sectors and contribute to sustainability transformation. Application Requirements Applicants must submit the following documents in English: Letter of Motivation (max. 6,500 characters) Highlight experience at the intersection of science and society Explain your vision and how you discovered the fellowship Project Proposal (max. 15,000 characters + references) Detailed plan of your proposed work Curriculum Vitae (CV) (max. 10,000 characters) Include career highlights and five key achievements Two Letters of Recommendation Must include referees’ contact details Applicants must also disclose any use of AI tools in preparing their application. Application Timeline Application Deadline: April 5, 2026 (11:59 PM CEST) Selection Announcement: Autumn 2026 Fellowship Start: Early 2027 Applications must be submitted via the Official Online Application Portal. Diversity and Inclusion Commitment RIFS strongly promotes diversity and equal opportunities. Applications are encouraged from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of: Nationality Gender identity Ethnicity Religion Age Physical ability Women and underrepresented groups are especially encouraged to apply. Why You Should Apply This fellowship offers a unique opportunity to: Work in a globally recognized sustainability research institute Access funding and resources to implement impactful projects Collaborate with experts across disciplines and sectors Contribute to shaping global sustainability solutions Download this position as a PDF. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. How long is the fellowship? The fellowship lasts up to one year and can be structured flexibly. 2. Is the fellowship fully funded? Yes, it includes a stipend, living cost support, and a project budget of up to €30,000. 3. Where is the fellowship based? In Potsdam, Germany, at the Research Institute for Sustainability (RIFS). 4. Can the fellowship be done remotely? No, fellows are expected to be on-site during their fellowship period. 5. Who is eligible to apply? Mid-career professionals with at least 10 years of experience in sustainability-related fields. 6. What kind of projects are expected? Projects that create long-term impact and bridge different sectors of society. 7. Is there an age limit? No specific age limit is mentioned. 8. When will results be announced? Selected fellows will be announced in autumn 2026. 9. Can applicants from any country apply? Yes, the fellowship is open globally. 10. What makes this fellowship unique? Its focus on transdisciplinary collaboration and long-term sustainability impact . Learn more about the Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship 2027 HERE. Click HERE to discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY. The post Klaus Töpfer Sustainability Fellowship 2027 in Germany: €30,000 Funded Opportunity for Global Sustainability Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: SAP Young Professionals Program Egypt 2026: The Ultimate Career Accelerator for African Graduates to Launch a High-Paying Tech Career
summary: If you are a recent graduate looking to break into the global technology industry, the SAP Young Professionals Program Egypt 2026 is one of the most powerful and transformative opportunities currently available. Designed to equip young talent with cutting-edge digital skills, globally recognized certifications, and direct pathways into employment, this program stands out as a career-defining initiative for aspiring professionals across Africa. What Is the SAP Young Professionals Program? The SAP Young Professionals Program is a fully funded, intensive training initiative delivered by SAP’s Digital Skills Center. It is specifically designed to transform recent graduates into job-ready SAP Associate Consultants, one of the most in-demand roles in enterprise technology. Participants undergo structured training in: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems Cloud computing technologies Data analytics and business intelligence Digital transformation strategies SAP software implementation and consulting The program combines technical instruction, real-world business scenarios, and professional development training, ensuring participants graduate with both hard and soft skills required in today’s job market. Why This Program Matters in 2026 As global organizations increasingly rely on enterprise software to manage operations, SAP remains a dominant force in the industry. This program is not just a training course—it is a gateway into a global ecosystem of employers, partners, and consulting firms. Graduates gain a competitive advantage by: Earning globally recognized SAP certifications Learning directly from industry experts Accessing exclusive job opportunities within the SAP ecosystem Building a professional network across Africa and beyond Although the program does not guarantee employment, it significantly increases employability by aligning participants with real market needs. Category of People Who Can Apply This opportunity is specifically designed for: Recent university graduates Early-career professionals Job seekers looking to enter the tech or business consulting field Individuals transitioning into digital or enterprise technology careers It is particularly ideal for: Graduates with limited or no work experience Candidates seeking international career exposure Young professionals interested in high-growth tech careers Eligible Countries and Regions The SAP Young Professionals Program Egypt 2026 primarily targets African candidates, with specific requirements depending on the country track. For the Egypt cohort: Applicants must have the legal right to work in Egypt The broader program is open to candidates from various African countries, depending on the specific cohort location Eligibility Criteria and Requirements To qualify for the program, applicants must meet the following criteria: Academic Qualifications Minimum of a Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field such as: Information Technology Computer Science Engineering Business Administration Management Information Systems Data Science or related disciplines Graduation Timeline Must have graduated within the last 1–3 years Strong academic performance (top quartile preferred) Employment Status Must be: Unemployed, OR Employed in a non-permanent or unrelated role Skills and Competencies Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Interest in SAP technologies and consulting careers Excellent communication and teamwork abilities Language Requirement Fluency in English (written and spoken) is mandatory Availability Full-time commitment required: Monday to Friday 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM during the program Technical Requirement Reliable internet connection for virtual training participation Program Structure and Learning Experience The program is delivered through a virtual live classroom format, ensuring accessibility while maintaining high-quality instruction. Participants will experience: Instructor-led training sessions Hands-on workshops and simulations Real-world case studies Collaborative group projects Continuous mentorship and coaching This immersive learning environment ensures that participants are not just trained—but transformed into industry-ready professionals. Key Benefits of the Program Joining the SAP Young Professionals Program comes with numerous advantages: 1. Globally Recognized Certification Participants earn SAP certifications that are highly valued by employers worldwide. 2. Career Pathway into SAP Consulting Graduates are introduced to job opportunities within SAP’s partner ecosystem. 3. Hands-On Experience Participants work on practical scenarios that mirror real business challenges. 4. Professional Development Training includes: Communication skills Interview preparation Workplace readiness 5. Networking Opportunities Connect with: SAP experts Industry leaders Fellow professionals across Africa 6. Free Training The program is completely free, removing financial barriers for participants. Important Notes The program does not guarantee a job, but significantly improves job prospects Applications are free of charge—avoid scams requesting payment Selection is competitive and based on merit How to Apply To apply for the SAP Young Professionals Program Egypt 2026: Visit the official SAP careers portal Search for “SAP Young Professionals Program Egypt 2026” Complete the online application form Submit required documents (CV, academic transcripts, etc.) Wait for screening and interview stages Apply here Deadline Not explicitly specified (applications are rolling; early application strongly recommended). The SAP Young Professionals Program Egypt 2026 is more than just a training initiative—it is a strategic career launchpad for ambitious African graduates. With the rapid growth of digital transformation across industries, SAP skills are becoming increasingly valuable, making this program a smart investment in your future. title: Moral Ambition Fellowships 2026–2027: Fully Funded Global Fellowship to Transform Your Career and Tackle the World’s Biggest Problems
summary: The The School for Moral Ambition has launched one of the most transformative global opportunities for professionals seeking meaningful, high-impact careers—the Moral Ambition Fellowships. These fellowships are not just another professional development program; they are a bold initiative designed to redirect talent toward solving the world’s most urgent and neglected problems. Unlike traditional fellowships that focus on academic research or short-term projects, this program is a fully funded, career-transition fellowship that empowers participants to leave conventional career paths and work directly on global challenges such as food systems reform, tax justice, and public health advocacy. What Are the Moral Ambition Fellowships? The Moral Ambition Fellowships are 7-month, full-time, paid programs that combine intensive training, real-world policy engagement, and placement in top global organizations. Key Features of the Fellowship Full-Time Commitment (7 Months) Fellows dedicate themselves entirely to impactful work for seven months. Paid Monthly Stipend Participants receive financial support to cover living costs while transitioning careers. 1 Month Intensive Training Focused on advocacy, policy influence, and systems change. 6 Months Placement Work directly with leading NGOs, think tanks, and advocacy organizations. Global Network Career Coaching Fellows gain access to mentorship, professional coaching, and long-term career opportunities. This structure ensures that participants do not just learn—but actively contribute to real-world change. Available Fellowship Programs (2026–2027 Cohorts) The program offers multiple specialized tracks across different regions and global issues: 1. Tax Fairness Fellowship Locations: Africa, USA, Canada, EU Focus: Global tax justice, inequality reduction, policy advocacy Timeline: September 2026 – March 2027 Status: Applications opening soon 2. Food System Reform Fellowship Location: USA Focus: Sustainable food systems, public health, environmental reform Timeline: September 2026 – March 2027 3. Tobacco-Free Future Fellowship Location: EU Focus: Public health advocacy and eliminating tobacco industry influence Timeline: September 2026 – March 2027 4. Food Transition Fellowship Location: EU Focus: Protein transition and sustainable agriculture Timeline: September 2026 – March 2027 5. University Fellowship (Harvard, USA) Target: Undergraduate students (specific cohorts) Timeline: Spring/Summer 2026 Who Can Apply? (Eligible Candidates) The fellowship is designed for mid-career professionals who want to pivot into high-impact work. Target Audience Professionals with 5–10+ years of experience Individuals in fields such as: Law Economics Policy and governance Consulting Communications and marketing Research and academia Ideal Candidate Profile Highly ambitious and impact-driven Willing to leave their current job temporarily Interested in solving global challenges Open to career transformation Importantly, prior experience in the specific fellowship topic is NOT required. Eligible Countries and Regions The program is globally inclusive, but eligibility depends on the fellowship track. General Eligibility Open to applicants from around the world Some fellowships require: Legal eligibility to work in EU, USA, or Canada Willingness to relocate temporarily if needed Regions Covered Africa Europe North America Global (for certain programs) This makes the fellowship particularly attractive for applicants from developing regions, including Africa. Fellowship Structure: A Deep Dive Phase 1: Intensive Training (Month 1) Participants undergo a high-level training program focused on: Policy advocacy Campaign strategy Systems thinking Real-world problem-solving This phase includes immersive learning experiences and collaborative sessions. Phase 2: Professional Placement (Months 2–7) Fellows are placed in top-tier organizations, where they: Work on live policy issues Conduct research and advocacy Engage with policymakers Build coalitions and networks This hands-on experience is a defining feature of the program. Phase 3: Career Acceleration After the fellowship, participants receive: Career coaching Networking opportunities Public speaking platforms Fundraising and leadership training Selection Process The fellowship uses a skills-based selection model , not just academic credentials. Application Stages Online application Skill assessment test (approx. 3 hours) Interview (case-based and behavioral) Final interview with host organization Competitiveness Hundreds of applicants compete for limited slots Example: 653 applicants for 22 positions in a previous cohort Apply here Benefits of the Moral Ambition Fellowship Financial Benefits Monthly stipend (approx. up to $5,000 depending on location) Professional Benefits Real-world policy experience Career transition support Access to global networks Personal Growth Purpose-driven work Leadership development Opportunity to influence global change Why This Fellowship Stands Out The Moral Ambition Fellowship is unique because it: Focuses on real-world impact, not theory Pays professionals to switch careers toward meaningful work Connects fellows directly to policy and decision-making spaces Targets neglected but solvable global problems It is ideal for individuals who feel their current careers lack purpose and want to contribute to solving global crises. Deadline 27th April 2026 If you are a professional with strong skills but feel disconnected from meaningful impact, this fellowship offers a rare opportunity to redefine your career and contribute to solving humanity’s biggest challenges. The Moral Ambition Fellowships are not just about career advancement—they are about aligning your work with global impact, purpose, and long-term change. title: Fully Funded University of Galway Scholarships 2026–2027 in Ireland
summary: If you are searching for one of the most prestigious study opportunities in Europe, the University of Galway Scholarships 2026–2027 in Ireland present a powerful pathway for students across the globe. These scholarships are designed to attract high-achieving individuals who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership potential, and a commitment to making a global impact. Why Choose University of Galway? The University of Galway is globally recognized for academic excellence and innovation. Ranked among the top 2% of universities worldwide, it offers a diverse range of scholarships aimed at empowering students to achieve their academic and professional goals. The institution provides funding opportunities across disciplines including science, engineering, business, arts, health sciences, and more. Types of University of Galway Scholarships The University offers multiple scholarship schemes tailored to different academic levels and applicant categories. 1. Undergraduate Scholarships These scholarships are designed to reward high-performing students entering bachelor’s degree programs. Academic Excellence Scholarships Creative Arts Scholarships Sports Excellence Scholarships These awards may include financial support, additional academic points, and access to exclusive development opportunities. 2. Postgraduate Scholarships (Master’s Level) Postgraduate students can access a variety of merit-based scholarships such as: Postgraduate Excellence Scholarship (€1,500) for EU students Business and Science Merit Scholarships Tuition fee reductions (up to 50% in some programs) These scholarships are highly competitive and based primarily on academic merit. 3. Research and PhD Scholarships For doctoral candidates, the university offers prestigious research funding options including: Hardiman Research Scholarships International PhD funding programs These scholarships typically cover tuition, stipends, and research costs. 4. International Student Scholarships The university offers Global Excellence Scholarships for outstanding international students. Available for undergraduate and postgraduate programs Focus on academic merit and leadership potential Some awards may reach up to €10,000 Scholarship Benefits Depending on the specific scholarship, benefits may include: Full or partial tuition fee coverage Monthly stipends (for research programs) Accommodation support Travel allowances Access to world-class research facilities Networking and career development opportunities Eligibility Criteria While requirements vary across programs, general eligibility includes: Academic Requirements Strong academic record (First-class or equivalent for postgraduate applicants) Admission offer from the University of Galway Demonstrated academic excellence and potential Additional Requirements Research potential (for PhD applicants) Extracurricular achievements (sports, arts, leadership) English language proficiency (if applicable) Eligible Countries and Regions These scholarships are open to a broad range of applicants: Non-EU Students: Africa, Asia, Middle East, Americas EU Students: Eligible for specific scholarships such as the Postgraduate Excellence Scholarship Global Applicants: Many scholarships are open worldwide This makes the opportunity highly inclusive and globally competitive. Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide To apply successfully, follow these steps: Step 1: Choose Your Program Select an eligible undergraduate, master’s, or PhD program at the University of Galway. Step 2: Apply for Admission Submit your application through the official university portal. Step 3: Secure an Offer Letter Most scholarships require you to first receive an admission offer. Step 4: Apply for Scholarships Some scholarships are automatic Others require a separate online application Step 5: Prepare Supporting Documents Step 5: Prepare Supporting Documents Typical requirements include: Academic transcripts Personal statement CV/Resume Reference letters Step 6: Submit Before Deadline Ensure all documents are submitted before the specified deadline. Apply here Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for: High-achieving students seeking international education African students looking for funded study abroad options Researchers aiming for world-class academic environments Individuals with leadership and innovation potential Key Tips to Increase Your Chances Apply early to maximize scholarship consideration Maintain excellent academic records Craft a strong personal statement Highlight leadership and extracurricular achievements Secure strong recommendation letters Important Deadlines (2026 Intake) Deadlines vary depending on the scholarship type. Below are some key dates: Hardiman PhD Scholarship: February 6, 2026 Global Excellence Scholarship Consideration: May 1, 2026 Postgraduate Excellence Scholarship: September 30, 2026 Undergraduate Postgraduate Merit Scholarships (various colleges): Around April 10, 2026 Some scholarships have no separate deadline and are awarded automatically upon admission. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Fully Funded University of Galway Scholarships 2026–2027 in Ireland appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Journalism Fellowship 2026: Cover Workplace Mental Health with NPF in Washington, D.C.
summary: Journalists looking to deepen their reporting on workplace mental health have an opportunity to gain specialized training through the Covering Workplace Mental Health Fellowship organized by the National Press Foundation . Scheduled for May 13–14, 2026, in Washington, D.C. , this two-day fellowship is designed to equip reporters with the tools and insights needed to cover one of the most pressing issues in today’s workforce. Program Overview As workplace dynamics continue to evolve—with trends such as AI-powered therapy tools, return-to-office mandates, shifts in the gig economy, and generational differences—mental health has become a critical topic for journalists across beats. This fellowship aims to support accurate, nuanced, and in-depth reporting on workplace mental health, including emerging treatments, policies, and research. The training will bring together selected business and health journalists for an intensive, in-person learning experience. Participants will engage directly with leading experts in organizational psychology and human resources, gaining valuable perspectives that can enhance their storytelling and reporting depth. What Participants Will Gain Fellows will have the opportunity to: Interact with subject-matter experts in workplace mental health, organizational psychology, and HR Learn best practices for reporting complex mental health issues in professional environments Hear firsthand insights from a recipient of the Carolyn C. Mattingly Award for Mental Health Reporting Strengthen their ability to produce impactful, evidence-based journalism Recharge professionally through a focused, collaborative learning environment This initiative reflects the foundation’s broader commitment to advancing high-quality journalism through skill-building and knowledge-sharing opportunities. Fellowship Benefits The National Press Foundation will cover: Round-trip airfare Hotel accommodation Select meals during the program This financial support ensures that selected fellows can fully participate without cost barriers. Eligibility Criteria The fellowship is highly competitive and open only to journalists based in the United States. Applicants from all media platforms—including print, digital, and broadcast—are encouraged to apply. The program places a strong emphasis on diversity and inclusion. Applications are particularly encouraged from: Journalists of color Reporters covering marginalized communities Staff from local and nonprofit news organizations It is important to note that while the fellowship addresses workplace mental health broadly, it is not specifically focused on mental health within newsrooms. Participation Requirements Attendance is mandatory for all sessions during the two-day program. Additionally, editors must commit to releasing selected journalists from their regular newsroom duties to allow full participation. Application Details Application Deadline: April 7, 2026 Program Dates: May 13–14, 2026 Location: Washington, D.C. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early due to the competitive nature of the program. Click Here To Apply Sponsors and Partners This fellowship is sponsored by the Luv U Project , with additional support from the American Psychological Association . The National Press Foundation retains full responsibility for the program’s editorial content. Speakers for the fellowship will be announced on a rolling basis as confirmations are finalized. This program presents a unique opportunity for journalists to expand their expertise and produce more informed, impactful coverage of workplace mental health—an issue that continues to shape the modern workforce. Discover similar opportunities here. The post Journalism Fellowship 2026: Cover Workplace Mental Health with NPF in Washington, D.C. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Ocean Literacy Training Courses 2026 (Free Online by UNESCO) – Learn Anytime, Anywhere
summary: The ocean is at the heart of life on Earth—and now, gaining knowledge about it has never been easier. The Intergovernmental Oceanographic Commission (IOC) of UNESCO invites learners worldwide to enroll in a series of free, self-paced Ocean Literacy training courses hosted on the OceanTeacher Global Academy (OTGA) platform. Designed for a global audience, these courses aim to empower individuals, communities, and professionals with the knowledge needed to support ocean sustainability and contribute to the UN Ocean Decade . 🌍 Why Ocean Literacy Matters Ocean literacy goes beyond understanding marine ecosystems—it connects human life, climate systems, and sustainable development. These courses equip participants with the tools to: Understand the ocean’s role in global sustainability Make informed environmental decisions Integrate ocean awareness into education, policy, media, and design Contribute to a healthier and more sustainable planet 📚 Available Ocean Literacy Courses 1. Why the Ocean Should Matter to Us All This introductory course builds foundational knowledge of ocean literacy and its relevance to everyday life. Duration: 10 hours Highlights: Ocean Literacy principles Ocean-human connections Introduction to the UN Ocean Decade 2. Mainstreaming Ocean Literacy in Governance A specialized course focusing on integrating ocean knowledge into policymaking and governance. Duration: 10 hours Highlights: Ocean sustainability challenges Policy tools and governance approaches Ecosystem services and public benefits 3. Educating About the Ocean: Waves of Change Tailored for educators, this course explores how to integrate ocean literacy into teaching practices. Duration: 12 hours Highlights: Teaching strategies and tools Formal and informal education methods Ocean sustainability education 4. iSea Stories: Ocean Literacy for Media Press Designed for media professionals, this course focuses on storytelling and communication. Duration: 10 hours Highlights: Ocean storytelling techniques Media strategies for sustainability Case studies and real-world examples 5. Ocean Literacy for Architects Urban Planners A unique course exploring the intersection of ocean sustainability and design. Duration: 10 hours Highlights: Ocean-conscious design principles Urban planning and marine ecosystems Sustainable infrastructure solutions 🎓 Key Benefits of the Program Completely free with open global access Self-paced learning —study anytime, anywhere No prerequisites required Interactive content : lectures, quizzes, case studies, webinars Certificate of completion upon finishing quizzes Learn directly from global experts in ocean science and sustainability 📝 How to Enroll Register on OceanExpert Wait for account approval Log into OceanTeacher Global Academy Self-enroll in your chosen course Use enrolment key: Ol 2026 APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more short courses opportunities, click here . 📅 Application Deadline All courses are open for enrollment until 31 December 2026 . 💻 Technical Requirements Computer (Windows or Mac) Updated web browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari) Stable internet connection Audio capability (speakers/headphones) 🌊 Start Your Ocean Learning Journey Today Whether you re a student, policymaker, educator, or creative professional, these courses offer a flexible and impactful way to deepen your understanding of the ocean and contribute to global sustainability efforts. Join thousands of learners worldwide and become part of the movement shaping the future of our oceans. title: Officially Green Leaders Fellowship 2026 in Nigeria: Fully Funded National Climate Leadership Programme for Emerging Sustainability Leaders
summary: The Officially Green Leaders Fellowship 2026 , convened by The Green Institute, is now open for applications. This prestigious, fully funded national programme is designed to identify, train, and empower one outstanding sustainability leader from each of Nigeria’s 36 states and the Federal Capital Territory (FCT). More than just a fellowship, this initiative is a national movement to institutionalize sustainability leadership , strengthen climate governance, and build a coordinated network of environmental changemakers across Nigeria. Through an intensive residential training and long-term action implementation, the programme equips fellows with the knowledge, tools, and networks needed to drive measurable environmental impact at the state level. About the Officially Green Leaders Fellowship The Officially Green Leaders Fellowship is a highly competitive programme that selects 37 exceptional individuals —one per state and the FCT—based on merit, leadership potential, and commitment to sustainability. The fellowship combines: A one-week immersive residential training Strategic collaboration and mentorship Development of actionable sustainability plans Long-term implementation of state-level climate initiatives At the end of the residency, each fellow becomes an Officially Green State Lead , responsible for executing a 12-month sustainability action blueprint in their respective state. Programme Structure and Focus Areas The fellowship is designed to provide a comprehensive and multidisciplinary learning experience across key sustainability domains. 1. Energy Transition and Green Innovation Participants will explore: Renewable and decentralized energy systems Circular economy models Sustainable enterprise development Climate finance and funding pathways 2. Climate Leadership and Governance This component focuses on: Climate science and policy frameworks Environmental governance at subnational levels Institutional engagement and policymaking processes Localization of Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) 3. State Green Action Blueprint Lab Fellows will develop: A 12-month sustainability roadmap Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and measurable targets Strategic partnership frameworks Monitoring and evaluation systems 4. Advocacy and Strategic Communication Training includes: Policy advocacy strategies Stakeholder engagement approaches Media positioning and public speaking Building influence across sectors 5. State Sustainability Cabinet Formation Each fellow will establish a State Sustainability Cabinet , comprising key leadership roles such as: Renewable Energy Lead Circular Economy Lead Climate Education Coordinator Biodiversity and Conservation Officer Community Engagement Director Research and Policy Analyst What the Fellowship Offers The programme is fully funded to ensure inclusive participation from all regions of Nigeria. Fully Funded Benefits Include: Full residential accommodation for the one-week training All meals and refreshments during the programme Transportation funding to and from the residency location This ensures that selection is based solely on merit, not financial capacity. Fellowship Outcomes and Impact Within 12 months of participation, the fellowship aims to achieve: Establishment of 37 State Sustainability Cabinets Implementation of state-level environmental initiatives Nationwide sustainability campaigns Strengthened youth-led climate advocacy Creation of a unified national sustainability ecosystem Participants will also contribute to a major national sustainability activation , positioning Nigeria as a leader in global climate action. Who Should Apply The programme is open to individuals who demonstrate: Proven commitment to sustainability or environmental action Leadership experience or strong leadership potential Ability to mobilize stakeholders at the state level Commitment to measurable, structured impact Applicants may come from: Civil society organizations Academia Public sector Private sector Grassroots initiatives Why You Should Apply The Officially Green Leaders Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to: Become a recognized sustainability leader in your state Gain advanced knowledge in climate policy and governance Build a network of national and global changemakers Drive real environmental impact through structured initiatives Application Timeline Application Deadline: August 26, 2026 Applications are competitive, and early submission is strongly encouraged. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the Official Fellowship Portal. Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. Is the Officially Green Leaders Fellowship fully funded? Yes, the fellowship covers accommodation, meals, and transportation. 2. Who is eligible to apply? Individuals across Nigeria with a strong commitment to sustainability and leadership. 3. How many fellows will be selected? A total of 37 fellows —one from each state and the FCT. 4. What happens after the residency? Fellows implement a 12-month sustainability action plan in their state. 5. Where will the residency take place? The location will be communicated to selected fellows. 6. What does the residency focus on? Climate leadership, governance, innovation, and sustainability strategy. 7. Can more than one person apply from a state? Yes, but only one fellow will be selected per state. 8. Is there an age limit? No specific age limit is stated. 9. When is the application deadline? August 26, 2026. 10. How will applicants be notified? Selected candidates will be contacted after the review process. 11. What makes this fellowship unique? It combines leadership training with state-level implementation and national coordination , creating long-term systemic impact. Learn more about the Officially Green Leaders Fellowship 2026 HERE. Click HERE to discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY and more African opportunities HERE. title: Changemaker Fellowship with Microsoft Elevate: Empowering Nonprofits Through AI
summary: In a rapidly evolving digital world, artificial intelligence is no longer a luxury—it’s a necessity for organizations striving to scale impact and remain sustainable. The Changemaker Fellowship , launched in collaboration with Microsoft Elevate, is a transformative opportunity designed specifically for nonprofit leaders and social impact professionals ready to harness the power of AI for good. This fellowship is built to equip organizations with cutting-edge AI strategies, technical skills, and personalized expert support to accelerate mission-driven work across communities worldwide. 🚀 What is the Changemaker Fellowship? The Changemaker Fellowship is a hands-on, high-impact program aimed at empowering nonprofits, charities, intergovernmental organizations (IGOs), and UN agencies to effectively adopt and implement AI solutions. Participants will gain: Practical AI knowledge tailored for social impact One-on-one coaching from AI experts Tools to automate workflows and improve operational efficiency Strategies to responsibly integrate AI into their organization Whether your goal is to increase outreach, improve service delivery, or build sustainable funding streams, this fellowship offers the roadmap and support to get there. 💡 Key Benefits of the Fellowship 1. Accelerate Your Mission Leverage AI tools to automate repetitive tasks and streamline operations, allowing your team to focus on high-impact initiatives that truly matter. 2. Strengthen Fundraising Donor Engagement Use data-driven insights to build personalized donor relationships, improve engagement, and create more reliable funding pipelines. 3. Modernize Secure Operations Adopt a next-generation tech stack that simplifies workflows, enhances data security, and increases organizational agility. 4. Build AI-Ready Teams Through hands-on coaching and technical training, your team will gain the skills needed to confidently adopt and manage AI technologies. 5. Ensure Responsible AI Use Establish clear guardrails and ethical frameworks to ensure safe, transparent, and accountable AI implementation. 🎯 Who Should Apply? This fellowship is ideal for: AI champions within nonprofits and social impact organizations Professionals in IT, operations, communications, and program management Forward-thinking leaders eager to drive AI adoption within their organizations If you are passionate about leveraging technology to create meaningful change, this program is designed for you. 📅 Important Dates Applications Open: April 22 Interest Registration: Now open Early registration is encouraged to stay ahead and receive updates as soon as applications go live. 🔗 How to Apply Interested candidates can register their interest here: APPLY HERE. Joining the interest list ensures you’ll be among the first to know when applications officially open. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . 🌱 Final Thoughts The Changemaker Fellowship represents a powerful step forward in bridging the gap between technology and social impact. By equipping organizations with AI-driven tools and strategies, it empowers leaders to scale their missions, optimize operations, and create lasting change in communities around the globe. If your organization is ready to embrace innovation and lead with purpose, this fellowship could be the catalyst for your next phase of growth. title: African Liberty Writing Fellowship 2026/2027: Paid Online Fellowship for Emerging African Writers and Thought Leaders
summary: The African Liberty Writing Fellowship 2026/2027 , powered by African Students For Liberty, is now open for applications. This highly competitive and fully online fellowship is designed to identify, train, and mentor young African writers who are passionate about public policy, governance, economics, and freedom-oriented ideas. With a structured training phase followed by a long-term writing fellowship, participants gain the opportunity to publish their work in top international media outlets, develop strong analytical writing skills, and build a global intellectual footprint. If you are an aspiring writer, student, or graduate eager to shape conversations around policy and society in Africa, this fellowship provides a powerful platform to amplify your voice. About the African Liberty Writing Fellowship The African Liberty Writing Fellowship is a flagship programme under African Students For Liberty, aimed at nurturing the next generation of African public intellectuals and policy writers. Through intensive training, mentorship, and real-world publishing opportunities, fellows are equipped with the skills needed to produce high-quality opinion articles, policy analyses, and thought leadership pieces. Fellows have previously been published in globally recognized platforms such as: Foreign Policy Al Jazeera Mail Guardian Programme Structure and Timeline The fellowship is divided into two major phases: 1. Training Phase (5 Weeks) Duration: May 25 – June 22, 2026 Intensive virtual training sessions Focus on writing, research, argumentation, and policy analysis 2. Writing Fellowship Phase Duration: July 2026 – July 2027 Long-term mentorship and writing development Continuous publication opportunities The entire programme is conducted fully online , making it accessible to participants across Africa and beyond. What the Fellowship Offers Selected fellows receive a combination of training, mentorship, and career development support: Key Benefits Paid fellowship (compensation based on performance assessments) Intensive training in opinion writing and policy analysis One-on-one mentorship from experienced editors Opportunity to publish in top international media outlets Global exposure and professional networking opportunities Development of a strong writing portfolio Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for the fellowship, applicants must: Be enrolled in or have graduated from an African institution of higher learning , or Be an African studying outside the continent The fellowship is open to individuals who demonstrate: Strong interest in writing, public policy, and governance Ability to think critically and communicate ideas effectively Commitment to completing the programme Application Process Applicants must complete the Online Application form through the official platform. Important Notes No application fee required No admission fee required Only one application per candidate is allowed Selection Process The programme is highly competitive: Approximately 1,900 applications were received in the previous cycle Around 60 candidates were selected for training Only 33 fellows were finally inducted Selection is based on: Writing ability and clarity of thought Analytical and critical thinking skills Motivation and commitment Application Deadline Deadline: April 30, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early due to the competitive nature of the fellowship. APPLY HERE Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) 1. Is this a paid fellowship? Yes, it is a paid fellowship. Compensation depends on performance during monthly assessments. 2. Is the programme online or in-person? The entire fellowship, including training and writing phases, is conducted fully online. 3. Is there an application or admission fee? No, both application and admission are completely free. 4. How competitive is the fellowship? It is highly competitive, with thousands of applicants and limited slots. 5. Can I apply if I am studying outside Africa? Yes, as long as you are an African pursuing higher education abroad. 6. When does the programme run? Training: May–June 2026 Fellowship: July 2026 – July 2027 7. What kind of writing will I do? Fellows produce opinion articles, policy analyses, and thought leadership content. 8. Do I need prior publication experience? Not necessarily, but strong writing skills are essential. 9. Who organizes the fellowship? The programme is organized by African Students For Liberty. 10. Who can I contact for inquiries? You can contact the Editor-in-Chief via email provided on the official platform. Why You Should Apply The African Liberty Writing Fellowship is more than just a training programme—it is a career-launching opportunity for aspiring African writers and thinkers. By participating, you will: Gain world-class writing and analytical skills Build a strong portfolio of published work Join a network of influential writers and policy thinkers Contribute to shaping conversations on Africa’s future Learn more about the African Liberty Writing Fellowship 2026/2027 HERE. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE and more African opportunities HERE.
